_comment: Manually tagged known high-pollution source zones in Delhi at H3 resolution 8. Traffic corridors sourced from Delhi Traffic Police density reports and CPCB CAAQMS station data. Industrial zones from DPCC (Delhi Pollution Control Committee) registered industrial cluster records.

properties.name: Anand Vihar Bus/Rail Terminal
properties.source: CPCB Anand Vihar station, Delhi Traffic Police, TomTom congestion index
properties.notes: One of Delhi's most polluted monitoring points. Heavy bus, truck and auto-rickshaw concentration. Consistently ranks highest in CPCB Delhi daily AQI bulletins.

properties.name: Punjabi Bagh residential-traffic corridor
properties.source: CPCB Punjabi Bagh station, Delhi Traffic Police Ring Road data
properties.notes: Ring Road intersection with high mixed-traffic volumes. CPCB station regularly records Very Poor AQI during winter months.

properties.name: Narela Industrial Area
properties.source: DPCC registered industrial units, CPCB Narela station
properties.notes: Narela is one of Delhi's designated industrial zones under the Master Plan. DPCC-registered units include electroplating, chemical, and light manufacturing. Brick kilns in the vicinity are additional PM2.5 sources.

properties.name: Bawana Industrial Estate
properties.source: DPCC Bawana Industrial Estate registry, CPCB station
properties.notes: Bawana is a planned industrial relocation zone for units shifted from residential areas. DPCC records show over 1,500 registered units. Significant NOx and PM2.5 contribution from foundries and chemical plants.

properties.name: R.K. Puram residential monitoring point
properties.source: CPCB R.K. Puram station, NH-48 corridor traffic data
properties.notes: Located near NH-48 and busy south Delhi arterials. High private vehicle and commercial traffic. CPCB station used as residential background reference.

properties.name: Mandir Marg central Delhi monitoring
properties.source: CPCB Mandir Marg station, central Delhi traffic surveys
properties.notes: Central Delhi monitoring point. Mix of government office traffic, commercial vehicles, and diesel generators. Used as urban background station in CPCB AQI reporting.
